focus: Collision check: determine whether opencode ships a built-in /goal command; decides Path A (rename to goal.md) vs Path B for the opencode_goal.md filename drift.

graphEvents.label: Does opencode ship a built-in /goal slash command forcing the opencode_ prefix?
No built-in /goal command exists in opencode 1.17.11 binary (collision rationale refuted)
The opencode_ prefix on the goal command avoids a namespace collision with a built-in /goal

label: No built-in /goal command exists in opencode 1.17.11 binary; collision-avoidance rationale for the opencode_ prefix is REFUTED. Path A (rename opencode_goal.md -> goal.md) is viable and preferred.

Shipped plugin fails its documented /goal invocation contract: no built-in /goal fallback + opencode_goal.md filename does not resolve to /goal under standard namespace rules -> the /goal set ... UX is currently uninvokable.
All 44 case-insensitive 'goal' strings in the opencode binary are non-command noise (common-word/TLD dictionary, system-prompt prose, Coq Goal and Lean4 goals grammar keywords).
Built-in opencode commands surface as plain Title-case name strings in the binary (Clear/Compact/Init/Update/Undo/Redo confirmed); Goal is absent.

direction: The opencode_ prefix is intentional collision avoidance with a built-in /goal command.
reason: No built-in /goal command token or Goal command-name string exists in opencode 1.17.11 (direct strings evidence).
